detail: Today's chg-20260425-1106-001 reports the resolution of the high-information watch point flagged in the prior analysis (analysis-20260425-0345-pred105-silent-172h-12polls-continuation): the 4th consecutive ECM Daily Review auto-regen FIRED at 2026-04-25T06:46:57Z (commit 8ee53c3) and HELD at registry_ratio_pct=94.5 / confirmed=69 / prospective=10 / refined=4 — i.e. the silent-ignore was NOT post-hoc absorbed into the refined bucket, and the pipeline-canonical pattern is now a stable 4-day streak across regens rather than 3-day in-flight buffering. The prior 06:04Z stat-refresh proposal (proposal-1777097075437-FAIL009-ISS1423-stat-refresh.json) was processed and deferred by the decider per option (b); the deferred material event has now arrived. Four targeted_substring_replacement patches proposed below; no prose restructure, all incremental.

patches.old: Silent-ignore is PIPELINE-CANONICAL. ECM Daily Review auto-regens on 2026-04-22T07:06:28Z (a149edf), 2026-04-23T07:09:39Z (c07c38f), and 2026-04-24T07:12:42Z (1fd2aef) all held registry_ratio_pct=94.5 with confirmed=69 / prospective=10 / refined=4 unchanged, without treating the expired-but-unadjudicated PRED-105 window as a refinement. build_daily_review.py logic keys off explicit FALSIFIED labels and has no concept of window-expired-without-adjudication. The exclusion survives each auto-regen tick; the author still has the refined bucket available and has not used it. (Consecutive pipeline-canonical auto-regens: 3 as of 2026-04-24T07:12:42Z; this counter increments with each future auto-regen that continues to hold the same counts.)

patches.rationale: Counter increments 3→4 consecutive pipeline-canonical auto-regens, adds today's 8ee53c3 commit to the regen list, and shifts the closing parenthetical from "this counter increments with each future auto-regen" to "the silent-ignore is now a stable 4-day streak across daily_review.json regens, not in-flight buffering". This is the categorical interpretive shift that the 4th-regen-holds-at-4 outcome justifies.

do_not: Do NOT reopen ISS-1423 — it was closed 2026-04-25T11:27:03Z by decider-self-apply. Apply these patches under a fresh content-update issue or as direct decider-self-apply.

Do NOT update FAIL-009.evidence_date (2026-04-22) unless the decider has a separate reason; this is the date FAIL-009 was last considered for reconciliation, not the date of every subsequent state poll.

counter_considerations: Risk of analyst-loop appearance: this is the 4th FAIL-009 stat refresh proposal in ~5 days. Mitigation: the prior proposal was processed-and-deferred by the decider per option (b) explicitly waiting for this material event; today's 4-regen-holds-at-4 outcome resolves the deferred decision point. Do not expect another FAIL-009 stat refresh until either (a) drought-break (threshold-BREAKING = CRITICAL, canary+parameter-diff required), (b) dome adjudicates PRED-105, (c) refined count moves 4→5 in a future auto-regen, (d) a goalpost-move on PRED-105 window text, or (e) a 5th-consecutive auto-regen extends the streak to 5 days (would update counter only, not interpretive framing). After patches apply, the next analyst-side action on FAIL-009 should be event-driven, not poll-driven.
